title: Poor Henry
content:
Thick in its glass
        The physic stands,
    Poor Henry lifts
        Distracted hands;
    His round cheek wans
        In the candlelight,
    To smell that smell!
        To see that sight!

    Finger and thumb
        Clinch his small nose,
    A gurgle, a gasp,
        And down it goes;
    Scowls Henry now;
        But mark that cheek,
    Sleek with the bloom
        Of health next week!
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Poor Henry"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
